zh-tw.title: 土味情話大全
zh-tw.description: 讓人又甜又尷尬的土味情話合集，撩人必備語錄

en.title: Cheesy Pickup Lines
en.description: A collection of cheesy yet charming pickup lines guaranteed to make them smile (or cringe)

zh-tw.content:
「你知道你跟星星有什麼不一樣嗎？」
「什麼？」
「星星在天上，你在我心裡。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 土味情話入門款，成功率中等
variations: 你知道你跟月亮有什麼不一樣嗎？月亮會變，我對你的心不會變

en.content:
"Do you know what's different about you and the stars?"
"What?"
"The stars are in the sky, but you're in my heart."
en.editorNote: Classic starter, moderate success rate
variations: What's different about you and the moon? The moon changes, but my feelings for you don't.

zh-tw.content:
「我覺得你好像一道數學題。」
「為什麼？」
「因為我對你完全沒有解。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 學生族群特愛這類型
variations: 你就像微積分，讓我越算越上頭

en.content:
"I think you're like a math problem."
"Why?"
"Because I have absolutely no solution for you."
en.editorNote: Students love this type
variations: You're like calculus - the more I work on you, the more obsessed I get.

zh-tw.content:
「你喜歡喝水嗎？」
「喜歡啊。」
「那你已經喜歡上七十幾percent的我了。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 理科生的撩人方式
variations: 你喜歡空氣嗎？喜歡啊。那你每一秒都在需要我了。

en.content:
"Do you like drinking water?"
"Sure."
"Then you already like about 70% of me."
en.editorNote: Science nerds' way of flirting
variations: Do you like breathing? Good — because I take your breath away, which means you need me to function.

zh-tw.content:
「你今天特別好看。」
「今天？那昨天呢？」
「昨天也是，明天也會是，每天都是。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 回答得很圓滑的示範
variations: 「你今天氣色很好。」「我昨天睡得不好耶。」「那你明天睡飽一定更好看，我現在就期待了。」

en.content:
"You look especially great today."
"Today? What about yesterday?"
"Yesterday too. And tomorrow. And every day."
en.editorNote: Smooth recovery example
variations: "You look great today." "I actually didn't sleep well last night." "Then tomorrow is going to be dangerous. I'm already worried for myself."

zh-tw.content:
你知道我最大的缺點是什麼嗎？

就是缺點你。
zh-tw.editorNote: 簡短有力的經典款
variations: 我有個缺點想改，就是缺少你的聯絡方式

en.content:
Do you know what my biggest shortcoming is?

Falling short of being with you.
en.editorNote: Short and punchy classic
variations: I have a flaw I'd like to fix: not having your number.

zh-tw.content:
「你是甜甜圈嗎？」
「為什麼？」
「因為我的生活遇到你，才算圓滿了。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 甜食愛好者最適合用這招，對方很難不笑
variations: 你是珍珠奶茶嗎？因為沒有你，生活就少了那個讓人開心的驚喜

en.content:
"Are you a donut?"
"Why?"
"Because my life finally feels complete now that you're in it."
en.editorNote: Works best with someone who has a sweet tooth — hard to keep a straight face
variations: Are you boba tea? Because without you, my day is missing that little surprise that makes it worth it.

zh-tw.content:
「你是螢幕保護程式嗎？」
「為什麼這樣問？」
「因為自從遇到你，我就捨不得鎖畫面了。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給愛滑手機的對象，效果加分
variations: 你是我手機的省電模式嗎？因為有你在，我什麼都不想做，只想看著你

en.content:
"Are you my screen saver?"
"What do you mean?"
"Because ever since I met you, I can't bring myself to lock the screen."
en.editorNote: Send this to someone who's always on their phone — extra points for irony
variations: Are you my low battery mode? Because with you around, I don't want to do anything else — just stay right here.

zh-tw.content:
「你知道你跟拼圖有什麼共同點嗎？」
「什麼？」
「沒有你，我這輩子都拼不完整。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 喜歡玩拼圖的對象超適用，也可以直接配上一張拼圖照片一起傳
variations: 你是我最後一塊拼圖——找了好久，原來你一直在這裡

en.content:
"Do you know what you have in common with a puzzle?"
"What?"
"Without you, I'll never be complete."
en.editorNote: Bonus points if you send this with a photo of a puzzle missing one piece
variations: You're my last puzzle piece — I searched everywhere, and you were here all along.

zh-tw.content:
「你信不信，只要你說一句話，我就知道你現在在想什麼？」
「好，你說說看。」
「你現在在想：『這個人怎麼這麼煩』，但你其實有一點點喜歡這種感覺。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 互動式情話，適合曖昧期試水溫，對方笑了就代表有戲
variations: 也可以用：「你現在在想——『這個人真的很無聊，但不知道為什麼我還是在這裡』」

en.content:
"Bet I know exactly what you're thinking right now."
"Oh yeah? Let's hear it."
"You're thinking: 'This person is so annoying.' But somewhere in there, you kind of enjoy it."
en.editorNote: Great for the flirty testing phase — if they laugh, that's a green light
variations: Alternate version: 'You're thinking: this person is ridiculous. And yet here you are, still reading this.'

zh-tw.content:
「你知道流星跟你有什麼共同點嗎？」
「什麼？」
「看到你的瞬間，讓人忍不住想許願。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 晚上看星星的時候說，效果翻倍，對方基本上沒有辦法不笑
variations: 你比流星還稀有——流星至少每年有固定的日子，但像你這樣的人，這輩子只遇得到一次

en.content:
"Do you know what you have in common with a shooting star?"
"What?"
"The moment I see you, I can't help but make a wish."
en.editorNote: Say this while stargazing for double effect — nearly impossible not to smile at
variations: You're rarer than a shooting star — they come around every year, but someone like you only happens once in a lifetime.

zh-tw.content:
「你喜歡吃棉花糖嗎？」
「還好啊。」
「那就是你的感覺——甜但不膩，讓人嚐一口就還想再一口。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 在甜點店或市集裡說，自然又不尷尬，對方多半會假裝翻白眼但心裡很開心
variations: 你比棉花糖還甜，但比它更難忘——棉花糖吃完就沒了，你讓我一直在想

en.content:
"Do you like cotton candy?"
"It's okay, I guess."
"That's exactly what you're like — sweet without being overwhelming. One taste and I always want more."
en.editorNote: Works best at a market or dessert shop — casual enough not to feel rehearsed
variations: You're sweeter than cotton candy, but way harder to forget — candy disappears, but you stay on my mind.

zh-tw.content:
「你是我的飛航模式嗎？」
「什麼意思？」
「因為只要跟你在一起，我就想把整個世界的訊號都關掉，只留下你。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給習慣低頭滑手機的對象，暗示你想要對方的全部注意力
variations: 你是我的勿擾模式——不是因為你讓我煩，是因為有你在，其他的通知都不重要了

en.content:
"Are you airplane mode?"
"What do you mean?"
"Because whenever I'm with you, I want to turn off every signal in the world and keep only you."
en.editorNote: Send to someone who's always on their phone — a gentle way of asking for their full attention
variations: You're my do-not-disturb mode — not because you're annoying, but because when you're here, nothing else needs my attention.

zh-tw.content:
「如果你今天找不到路，往我這個方向走就好。」
「你知道路嗎？」
「不知道。但我知道我在這裡等你，不管你從哪個方向來。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合傳給迷路（字面意義或心情上）的另一半，安心感十足
variations: 我不確定前面的路怎麼走，但我確定我想跟你一起走過去

en.content:
"If you ever feel lost today, just walk toward me."
"Do you know the way?"
"No. But I know I'll be here waiting, no matter which direction you come from."
en.editorNote: Send this when your partner is literally lost, or just having an overwhelming day — works for both
variations: I don't know what's ahead of us. But I know I want to walk there with you.

zh-tw.content:
「你是美式咖啡嗎？」
「為什麼這樣問？」
「因為不管我加幾顆糖，你永遠讓我想再多喝一口。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 在咖啡廳說效果最好，或是傳給喜歡喝咖啡的人，附上一杯咖啡的照片更加分
variations: 你是拿鐵嗎？因為有你在，生活的苦都變得順口多了

en.content:
"Are you an Americano?"
"Why do you ask?"
"Because no matter how much sweetness I add to my life, I always want another cup of you."
en.editorNote: Works best at a coffee shop, or send with a photo of a coffee cup for extra points
variations: Are you a latte? Because with you around, even the bitter parts of life go down smooth.

zh-tw.content:
「你知道你跟Google地圖有什麼共同點嗎？」
「什麼？」
「每次我不知道該往哪裡走，只要想到你，我就知道方向了。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合傳給在人生路口感到迷惘的對象，或是在旅途中傳，讓對方秒懂你的心意
variations: 你是我的導航——不是因為我迷路，是因為有你在的方向，我都想去

en.content:
"Do you know what you have in common with Google Maps?"
"What?"
"Every time I don't know where I'm going, just thinking about you tells me exactly which direction to walk."
en.editorNote: Send while traveling together, or when your partner is at a crossroads in life
variations: You're my GPS — not because I'm lost, but because wherever you are is exactly where I want to go.

zh-tw.content:
「你知道今天天氣預報說什麼嗎？」
「說什麼？」
「說你出現的地方，有100%機率感覺特別暖。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 天氣很冷或很熱的時候都能用，通用款土味情話，對方多半會先白眼再笑
variations: 氣象局沒辦法預測你出現的時間，但每次你出現，天氣就變好了

en.content:
"Do you know what today's weather forecast says?"
"What does it say?"
"That wherever you show up, there's a 100% chance it suddenly feels warm."
en.editorNote: Works in any weather — a universally applicable cheesy line; expect an eye roll followed by a smile
variations: The weather app can't predict when you'll show up. But every time you do, the forecast improves.

zh-tw.content:
「你像一本書嗎？」
「哪種書？」
「那種翻了第一頁就放不下，到了最後一頁才發現時間不知道跑去哪了的書。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給愛讀書的對象最有加分效果，也適合搭配一本對方喜歡的書一起說
variations: 你是我想一直讀下去的那一種書——讀完了也不想還回去

en.content:
"Are you like a book?"
"What kind?"
"The kind where after the first page, I can't put you down — and when I finally look up, I don't know where the time went."
en.editorNote: Extra points if your partner loves to read — pair it with a book they mentioned wanting to try
variations: You're the kind of book I want to keep reading forever — and never return to the shelf.

zh-tw.content:
「你有沒有一首歌，聽著聽著就很想傳給某個人？」
「有啊。」
「我也有，而且不管聽什麼歌，第一個想傳的都是你。
還沒找到一首不想傳給你的。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給喜歡的人，搭配一首歌一起傳效果加倍，對方通常會問「是哪首歌」
variations: 你是我的單曲循環——不是因為我不想換，是因為我一直覺得你比任何歌都好聽

en.content:
"Do you ever hear a song and immediately want to send it to someone?"
"Yeah, sometimes."
"I have that too. Except no matter what song I hear, the first person I want to send it to is always you.
Haven't found one I don't want to share with you yet."
en.editorNote: Send this with a song attached for double the effect — they'll probably ask which one
variations: You're my song on repeat — not because I can't find another, but because nothing sounds better than you.

zh-tw.content:
「你知道你讓我困擾的地方是什麼嗎？」
「什麼？」
「就是你不在的時候，我連手機都覺得沒什麼好看的。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給習慣一直滑手機的對象，讓他知道你比手機更讓他在意
variations: 你是我關閉所有通知的理由——不是因為你打擾我，是因為有你才叫做真正的通知

en.content:
"Do you know what the most annoying thing about you is?"
"What?"
"When you're not around, I can't even find anything worth looking at on my phone."
en.editorNote: Send to someone who's always on their phone — it's a compliment and a gentle nudge at once
variations: You're the reason I turned off all my notifications — not because you bother me, but because you're the only alert that matters.

zh-tw.content:
「你知道我最討厭什麼天氣嗎？」
「什麼？」
「沒有你的天氣。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 簡短有力的經典款，不管什麼天氣都能用，對方第一反應通常是翻白眼，第二反應是笑
variations: 你是我的天氣預報——不管今天幾度，只要你在，就是晴天

en.content:
"Do you know what kind of weather I hate the most?"
"What?"
"The kind without you in it."
en.editorNote: Short and effective — works in any weather. Expect an eye roll, then a smile.
variations: You're my weather forecast — no matter what the temperature is, it's sunny when you're around.

zh-tw.content:
「你昨晚夢到什麼？」
「忘了。你呢？」
「我夢到你了。
然後早上醒來，我才發現不是夢——
因為我馬上就想要傳訊息給你了。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 早上傳效果最好，讓對方一起床就有好心情，曖昧期特別好用
variations: 就算你沒在我夢裡，我一睜開眼第一個想到的還是你——夢不夢到已經不重要了

en.content:
"What did you dream about last night?"
"I forgot. You?"
"I dreamed about you.
Then I woke up and realized it wasn't a dream —
because the first thing I wanted to do was message you."
en.editorNote: Send in the morning for maximum effect — starts their day on a warm note. Great for the flirty phase.
variations: Even if you don't show up in my dreams, you're still the first thing I think of when I open my eyes — being in the dream doesn't even matter.

zh-tw.content:
「你知道你跟春天有什麼共同點嗎？」
「什麼？」
「你一出現，我就覺得冬天終於過了。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 換季的時候說效果特別好，也適合在對方心情不好、剛走出低潮的時候傳
variations: 你比春天更準時——春天有時候會遲，但你出現的那一刻，我立刻就暖了

en.content:
"Do you know what you have in common with spring?"
"What?"
"Every time you show up, I feel like winter finally ended."
en.editorNote: Perfect at the start of a new season, or when your person is coming back from a rough patch — simple and warm
variations: You're more reliable than spring — spring sometimes runs late, but you? The warmth hits the moment you arrive.

zh-tw.content:
「你是火鍋嗎？」
「為什麼？」
「因為天冷的時候，我第一個想到的就是你。
然後就覺得暖了。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 冬天或天氣變冷的時候傳，配上一張火鍋的照片效果加倍，不做作又暖心
variations: 你是薑母鴨嗎？因為有你在，我連最冷的夜都覺得可以撐過去

en.content:
"Are you a bowl of hot pot?"
"Why?"
"Because when the weather gets cold, you're the first thing I think of.
And then I'm warm."
en.editorNote: Perfect in winter or on a rainy day — pair with a photo of hot pot for extra effect. Cozy, not overdone.
variations: Are you a warm cup of tea? Because just knowing you exist makes the coldest nights feel manageable.

zh-tw.content:
「你知道你讓我特別安心的地方是什麼嗎？」
「什麼？」
「就是不管我今天過得有多糟，只要你的名字出現在螢幕上，我就自動好了一半。
我也不知道為什麼，但已經習慣了。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給讓你安心的人，讓他知道他對你來說有多重要，比說「我喜歡你」更日常、更有感
variations: 你的訊息是我一天裡最不需要想太多的事——看到了就開心，不需要理由

en.content:
"Do you know what it is about you that makes me feel calm?"
"What?"
"No matter how rough my day is, the moment your name shows up on my screen, I'm already halfway okay.
I don't fully understand it. But I've gotten used to it."
en.editorNote: Send to the person who makes you feel settled — it says more than 'I like you' without being too much
variations: Your messages are the one thing in my day that I don't have to think about. I see your name. I'm happy. No explanation needed.

zh-tw.content:
「你知道你讓我想到什麼嗎？」
「什麼？」
「那種咖啡廳裡偶爾會遇到的貓。
牠不理你，但如果牠突然多看你一眼，
你就覺得這一天值了。
你就是那種程度的存在。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個你默默很在乎、但不太確定對方感覺的人，帶點俏皮但真心
variations: 換個說法：你是讓我無論如何都想靠近、但靠近了又不知道說什麼的那一種人

en.content:
"Do you know what you remind me of?"
"What?"
"That cat you sometimes find in a coffee shop.
It doesn't acknowledge you, but if it glances your way — just once —
your whole day feels worth it.
You're exactly that kind of presence."
en.editorNote: Send to someone you care about quietly — playful but sincere, great for the uncertain-feelings phase
variations: Another angle: you're the person I always want to get closer to, and then when I do, I don't know what to say.

zh-tw.content:
「你知道你跟雨傘有什麼共同點嗎？」
「什麼？」
「最需要的時候，我第一個想到的就是你。
而且只要你在，我就覺得今天不管下什麼，都沒有那麼嚴重了。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 下雨天傳效果最好，讓對方感覺自己是你的避風港，不做作又暖心
variations: 你是我的備案，也是我的第一選擇——不一樣的地方在於，備案是退而求其次，你從來不是。

en.content:
"Do you know what you have in common with an umbrella?"
"What?"
"When I need one most, you're the first thing I reach for.
And with you around, whatever the day throws at me doesn't feel so serious anymore."
en.editorNote: Perfect to send on a rainy day — tells them they're your shelter without being dramatic
variations: You're both my backup plan and my first choice — the difference being, a backup is a compromise. You never are.

zh-tw.content:
「你知道我記憶力很不好嗎？」
「真的嗎？」
「對，會忘東忘西。但你說的話，我一句都沒忘過。
我也不知道為什麼，就是記得特別牢。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合傳給那個說過你不記得細節的另一半，讓他知道你其實有在聽，只是選擇性地留住了最重要的那些
variations: 你說的每一句話，有沒有覺得我記得比你想的多？不是故意的，是你說的太值得記住了。

en.content:
"You know I have a terrible memory, right?"
"Really?"
"Yeah, I forget things all the time. But I haven't forgotten a single thing you've ever said to me.
I'm not sure why. They just stuck."
en.editorNote: Send to a partner who thinks you never listen — shows them you do, just selectively remember what matters most
variations: Have you noticed I remember more of what you've said than you'd expect? It's not intentional. What you say is just worth holding onto.

zh-tw.content:
「你喜歡吃湯圓嗎？」
「還好啊，為什麼？」
「因為有你在，圓滿這件事我早就懂了。
湯圓只是提醒我一下，原來這種感覺早就有個名字。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 冬至或元宵節傳效果最好，也適合配上一張湯圓照片一起傳，節慶感十足又不失溫度
variations: 你是我的湯圓——甜的，圓的，而且不管什麼時候出現，都讓這個節日多了意義。

en.content:
"Do you like sweet rice balls?"
"They're okay — why?"
"Because with you around, I already know what completeness feels like.
The rice balls just reminded me there's been a name for it all along."
en.editorNote: Perfect around Lantern Festival or Winter Solstice — send with a photo of the food for full seasonal effect
variations: You're my sweet rice ball — round, sweet, and no matter when you show up, the day suddenly has more meaning.

zh-tw.content:
「你知道化學裡的催化劑是什麼嗎？」
「讓反應更快發生的東西？」
「對，而且它自己不會消耗掉，但沒有它，很多事情都不會開始。
你對我來說，就是那種存在。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 理科背景的對象看到這個會先確認定義正確，然後才意識到是情話，時間差讓它加倍有趣
variations: 你是我生活反應的催化劑——不是主角，但如果沒有你，什麼都不會發生得那麼順。

en.content:
"Do you know what a catalyst is in chemistry?"
"Something that makes reactions happen faster?"
"Right. And it doesn't get used up itself — but without it, a lot of things would never start.
That's exactly what you are to me."
en.editorNote: A science-minded partner will first verify the chemistry definition, then slowly realize it's a pickup line — the delayed reaction makes it funnier
variations: You're the catalyst in my life's reactions — not the main element, but without you, nothing happens as smoothly.

items.sourceNote: MBTI I人/E人情話，重新創作

variations: I人反向版：「你是E人嗎？因為你讓我願意主動傳訊息——這對我來說幾乎算是奇蹟。」

en.content:

"Extrovert, probably — why do you ask?"
"Because the moment you walked in,
my internal monologue switched from silent mode to full speaker.
And I still haven't turned it off."
en.editorNote: Great opener for new connections — MBTI is huge with younger Taiwan audiences and this lands as both on-trend and a little sweet
variations: Introvert version: 'Are you an extrovert? Because you made me want to text first — which for me is basically a miracle.'
I used to be very much an introvert. But whenever you show up, I suddenly have a lot to say. Is that what E feels like?

items.sourceNote: 朋朋情話，重新創作

主動權在你。

我等。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 「朋朋」是2026台灣Z世代流行的親暱稱呼，這句情話輕鬆、可愛，不會太有壓力，很適合曖昧期試探
variations: 進階版：「朋朋，我覺得我們可以upgrade一下稱呼。你覺得什麼時候開始叫不一樣的？」

"Hey, can I call you the 'friend' in 'boyfriend' or 'girlfriend' today?"
"What do you mean?"
"Let's just start with 'friend.' We can add the rest later.
The decision is yours.

I'll wait."
en.editorNote: Casual, cute, low-pressure — perfect for the uncertain flirting phase when you like someone but haven't made it official
variations: Advanced version: 'I think we're ready to upgrade what we call each other. When do you want to start?'

items.sourceNote: I人勇敢主動情話，重新創作

zh-tw.content:

通常要在心裡考慮很久很久嗎？

zh-tw.editorNote: I人的告白方式，讓對方知道每一次主動對你來說都有意義——比「我喜歡你」更讓人心動的是「你讓我願意先開口」
variations: E人對I人說：「你每次傳訊息給我，我都知道那不簡單——所以我每一則都很認真看。」

en.content:
"You know I'm an introvert, right?"
"Yeah."
"Then you know it usually takes me a long, long time
to decide to reach out first.

So the fact that you're getting messages from me?
Means you're worth overcoming myself for."
en.editorNote: An introvert's perfect confession — instead of 'I like you,' this says 'you make me brave enough to reach out first.' That lands harder.
variations: If you're an extrovert saying this to an introvert: 'Every time you text me first, I know it wasn't easy — so I read every single one carefully.'

items.sourceNote: 旅遊等待情話，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
「你知道你讓我想到什麼嗎？」
「什麼？」

zh-tw.editorNote: 規劃旅遊的時候說，或是傳給你一直想約出去玩的人，讓他知道你一直在等他
variations: 你是那種讓我想買機票的人——不是因為目的地，是因為你。

en.content:
"Do you know what you remind me of?"
"What?"
"A place I've always wanted to go but never been — not because I was afraid to go alone, but because I was waiting for the right person to go with."
en.editorNote: Say this while planning a trip, or send to someone you've been wanting to invite out — let them know they're the reason you've been waiting
variations: You're the kind of person that makes me want to buy a plane ticket — not for the destination, but for you.

items.sourceNote: 被你說話迷住的情話，重新創作

variations: 你說話的時候，我沒辦法想其他事情。這不是分心，這是你佔據了全部。

en.content:

"What do you mean?"
"I had something to say. Then you started talking. And I just wanted to keep listening.
Whatever I was going to say probably wasn't that important anyway."
en.editorNote: Say this mid-conversation — let them pause, then smile
variations: When you talk, I can't think about anything else. That's not distraction. That's you taking up the whole space.

items.sourceNote: 引力情話理科版，重新創作

variations: 如果我是一個天體，你就是讓我繞著你轉的那個力場。物理上來說，這樣的軌道相當穩定。

"Right — exactly like you. I can't explain why, but every time you show up, I drift in your direction.
I think Newton was describing something like this."
en.editorNote: A science-minded person will first verify the physics definition, then slowly realize it's a pickup line — the delay makes it twice as effective
variations: If I were a celestial object, you'd be the gravitational field that keeps me in orbit. Physically speaking, that's quite a stable trajectory.

items.sourceNote: 麻薯食物情話，重新創作

zh-tw.content:
「你喜歡吃什麼口味的麻薯？」

variations: 你比任何甜點都更讓人忍不住——甜點吃多了會膩，你不會。

I'm not sure why. I just can't help it."
en.editorNote: Works best at a dessert shop or while eating snacks — natural context, no awkwardness. Expect an eye roll, then the mochi pushed toward you.
variations: You're more addictive than any dessert — sweets eventually feel like too much. You never do.

items.sourceNote: WiFi connection line，重新改寫為對話體情話

zh-tw.content:

en.editorNote: Works best in a café, library, or any public space — casual setting makes it land naturally; expect a pause, then a smile
variations: You're the WiFi I automatically connect to — I didn't even ask for the password. I just knew this was the right place.

items.sourceNote: library check-out line，重新改寫為更有層次的對話體

en.editorNote: Use at a library, bookstore, or with a bookworm — playful but sincere, hard not to laugh at the bureaucratic framing
variations: You're the kind of book I'd never return — even after the overdue notice.

items.sourceNote: gym weak-in-the-knees line，重新改寫為台灣口語情話

But every time I see you, my legs just go weak on their own.
I think that counts as a more efficient workout than squats."
en.editorNote: Perfect at the gym or during any workout — expect a laugh and a 'you're ridiculous,' which means it worked
variations: You're the training session I never want to skip — not because it's exhausting, but because you're always worth coming back for.

items.sourceNote: alphabet/letter wordplay line，重新改寫為中英文雙版情話

items.sourceNote: mirror reflection pickup lines，重新改寫為對話體情話

en.editorNote: Compliments their looks without saying 'you're beautiful' directly — the misdirect makes it memorable; expect a pause then a laugh

items.sourceNote: shadow presence romantic line，重新改寫為台灣口語情話

No calibration needed."

我現在有點擔心你。」

items.sourceNote: timezone distance romantic line，重新改寫為台灣口語情話

時差跟我沒有辦法談判。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 特別適合異地或不同時區的兩個人，讓對方知道你想他的時間不受任何條件限制，比「我很想你」更有畫面
variations: 你讓我第一次覺得，時區這件事真的很多餘——不管相差幾小時，想你的時間點都是一樣的。

"You know what's wild about time zones?"

But no matter what time it is for me, the moment I want to text you — that's the time.
Time zones have no leverage over that."
en.editorNote: Perfect for long-distance or different-time-zone situations — more vivid than 'I miss you,' tells them your thoughts don't follow a schedule
variations: You're the first person who made me feel like time zones are completely unnecessary — no matter the gap, the moment I think of you is always the same.

variations: 進階版：「那我能叫你朋朋嗎？」「朋朋是什麼意思？」「就是我的男女朋友的朋，叫兩次。」

That way, every time I say 'friend-friend,'

variations: Advanced: 'Can I call you friend-friend?' 'What does that mean?' 'It means my boyfriend/girlfriend's peng, said twice.'

Because the moment you walked in,
my deeply introverted inner voice
silently started warming up.

variations: Simplified: 'I'm an introvert. But you make my inner voice want to talk.'

variations: 更直接版：「你對我施了硬控，我需要你來解除它。」

Can't look away,
can't walk away,
absolutely stuck.

I think this debuff is your responsibility to remove.

variations: More direct: 'You've applied a hard-control effect on me. You need to come remove it.'

items.sourceNote: astrology pickup lines inspiration; rewritten as Taiwan-style 土味情話 with zodiac-manifestation angle

My horoscope today said I'd meet someone who makes my heart skip a beat.
I thought it was just the usual vague stuff.
But now I kind of owe that astrologer an apology."
en.editorNote: Zodiac talk is an easy icebreaker with younger crowds — framing meeting them as a horoscope come true is funny and disarming. Expect an eye roll followed by 'so what's YOUR sign?'
variations: You know what my horoscope said today? That someone would completely derail my focus. I think it was describing you. Which technically makes me a psychic.

items.sourceNote: Netflix binge pickup line; rewritten as conversational 土味情話 with local streaming culture angle

zh-tw.editorNote: 從追劇這個超日常的話題切入，把對方說話的方式比喻成讓人上癮的劇，讓對方很難直接回答你本來的問題——因為他已經在笑了
variations: 你是那種我不想跳片頭曲的劇——通常我都直接按跳過，但你出現的每一段，我都想重看一次。

en.content:
"Are you watching any shows lately?"
"Yeah, I've been watching —"
"Got it.
But here's the thing — the way you talk is more binge-worthy than anything in my watchlist.
I'm a little worried you might become a harder habit to break than any series I've ever watched."
en.editorNote: Starting with a completely normal question about shows, then pivoting to the compliment, catches them off guard. They'll forget what show they were about to name.
variations: You're the kind of show where I don't skip the intro — I usually fast-forward everything, but every moment you're in, I want to replay.

items.sourceNote: keyboard/typing 'just my type' classic; rewritten as zh-tw wordplay using 打字/心動 angle

zh-tw.content:
「你打字很快嗎？」
「還好啊。」
「那你應該知道，有些字打了很多次，手會自動記住。
就像你的名字——
我傳訊息的時候，輸入法打一半就出來了。
我想我的手比我更早知道這件事。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 把手機輸入法的「學習功能」變成一個迂迴的告白，說對方的名字已經被手記住了——比直接說「我一直想到你」更有溫度，也更有記憶點
variations: 我手機的輸入法最近學得很快——只要我開始打，你的名字就第一個出來。它比我更老實。

en.content:
"Do you type fast?"
"More or less."
"Then you know how some words get typed so often, your hands just remember them on their own.
Like your name —
half the time, autocomplete fills it in before I even finish.
I think my hands figured it out before I did."
en.editorNote: Turning autocomplete into a confession — saying your hands already know their name by heart is warmer and more unexpected than 'I think about you all the time'
variations: My keyboard's been learning fast lately — whenever I start typing, your name is always the first suggestion. It's more honest than I am.

items.sourceNote: CPR/breath classic; rewritten as medical-comedic dialogue with fresh twist, avoiding direct copy

zh-tw.content:
「你會急救嗎？」
「有基本概念，怎麼了？」
「因為你一出現，我就忘了怎麼呼吸了。
我覺得你是我唯一的解方，
但這樣說起來你造成的問題跟解決的問題一樣多。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 把「讓我喘不過氣」的感覺包裝成急救問題，比直接說「你讓我心跳加速」更有笑點，對方通常會先愣一下然後笑說「你怎麼這樣」
variations: 你知道心肺復甦術是幹嘛的嗎？因為每次遇到你，我都要重新學一次怎麼呼吸。

en.content:
"Do you know first aid?"
"The basics, yeah. Why?"
"Because every time you show up, I forget how to breathe.
You feel like the only remedy.
Although technically you're causing as many problems as you're solving."
en.editorNote: Wraps the breathless feeling in a mock-medical scenario — funnier and more original than the straightforward version. The logical twist at the end is what makes it land.
variations: Do you know what CPR is for? Because every time I run into you, I have to relearn how to breathe from scratch.

items.sourceNote: plant care / nurturing metaphor angle; rewritten as Taiwan-style 土味情話 with 澆水 daily routine framing

zh-tw.content:
「你有在養植物嗎？」
「有啊，為什麼？」
「因為我最近發現，有些東西需要每天澆水才能長好。
我現在很想固定傳訊息給你。
我覺得這兩件事的道理是一樣的。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 把「想每天聯絡你」說成澆水養植物，比直接說「我想每天傳訊息給你」更有生活感，適合傳給喜歡植物或生活感強的對象
variations: 你是那種養了就捨不得不管的植物——不是因為麻煩，是因為每天看著它長大，這件事本身就讓我很開心。

en.content:
"Do you keep any plants?"
"Yeah, why?"
"Because I've been noticing that some things need a little attention every day to grow well.
I've been feeling a strong urge to text you every morning.
I think those two things are connected."
en.editorNote: Comparing daily texting to watering a plant is warmer and more original than 'I want to talk to you every day' — works especially well with someone who loves plants or cozy daily routines
variations: You're the kind of plant I'd never forget to water — not out of obligation, but because checking on you is the part of the day I actually look forward to.

items.sourceNote: sunset golden hour romantic angle; rewritten as Taiwan-style 土味情話 dialogue avoiding direct translation

zh-tw.content:
「你有沒有遇過那種，看著夕陽捨不得眨眼的時候？」
「有啊。」
「就是怕眨一下，它就不見了。
我看你也有同樣的問題。
你在的時候，我眨眼都覺得可惜。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 把「捨不得移開視線」說成夕陽的感覺，詩意但不做作，黃昏或夕陽下說效果翻倍，是那種對方聽完會沉默一秒再笑的情話
variations: 夕陽只有幾分鐘，但我跟你說話這件事，我從來不覺得夠。

en.content:
"Have you ever watched a sunset and been afraid to blink?"
"Yeah."
"Because you're scared one blink is all it takes for it to be gone.
I have the same problem when you're around.
Every time I blink, I feel like I'm missing something."
en.editorNote: Framing 'I can't stop looking at you' through the lens of a sunset makes it poetic without being overdone — say this during golden hour for full effect; expect a quiet pause, then a smile
variations: Sunsets only last a few minutes. But talking to you is the one thing that never feels like enough time.

items.sourceNote: gaming rare drop / SSR loot metaphor; rewritten as Taiwan gaming slang 土味情話 with 稀有度 framing

zh-tw.content:
「你玩手遊嗎？」
「偶爾，為什麼？」
「因為你是那種SSR。
我沒有充一毛錢，就這樣遇到你了。
我到現在還覺得這個機率有點不真實。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 把「遇到你」說成抽到SSR稀有角色，深受遊戲玩家歡迎，也適合不玩遊戲但懂梗的人——聽完通常會說「你在幹嘛」然後笑出來
variations: 你知道有些角色是課再多金也不一定能拿到的嗎？你就是那種——我沒花任何代價，就這樣碰到你了。

en.content:
"Do you play mobile games?"
"Sometimes, why?"
"Because you're like an SSR character.
I didn't spend a single cent, and somehow you just showed up.
Honestly, the odds still don't feel real to me."
en.editorNote: Framing meeting them as pulling an SSR (super rare character) without spending money — a natural fit for anyone who games, but the logic lands even for non-gamers; expect 'what is wrong with you' followed by a laugh
variations: Some characters you can pour money into a game and still never get. You're that kind — and somehow I got you without trying.

items.sourceNote: morning alarm / snooze daily routine angle; rewritten as Taiwan-style 土味情話 with 賴床 framing

zh-tw.content:
「你是那種鬧鐘一響就起床，還是一直按掉的人？」
「一直按掉的那種。」
「我也是。但你傳訊息來的那種，我每次都秒讀。
我自己也覺得很驚訝，就這樣。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 從賴床這個超日常的習慣切入，把「你的訊息我一定秒回」說得很自然，不刻意卻很有溫度，早上傳效果最好
variations: 我每天最難的事是從床上起來。但你傳訊息來的那一秒，這件事突然變得很容易。

en.content:
"Are you the type who jumps out of bed when your alarm goes off, or the type who keeps hitting snooze?"
"Definitely the snooze type."
"Same. But when you send me a message — I open it instantly, every time.
Even I'm a little surprised by that, honestly."
en.editorNote: Starting with a totally relatable morning habit makes the pivot feel natural rather than rehearsed — works best sent in the morning; the self-aware ending lands especially well
variations: Getting out of bed is the hardest thing I do every day. But the moment you message me, that suddenly becomes easy.

items.sourceNote: fingerprint / unlock phone uniqueness angle; rewritten as Taiwan-style 土味情話 with 指紋辨識 framing

zh-tw.content:
「你知道指紋辨識是怎麼運作的嗎？」
「就是辨識你自己的指紋？」
「對，全世界沒有兩個一樣的。
你對我來說也是這樣——
我沒有辦法用其他人的名字替換掉你那個位置。
不相容。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 把「你是獨一無二的」說成指紋辨識的邏輯，比直接說「你很特別」更有記憶點，理工背景的對象或常滑手機的人聽到這個特別有感
variations: 你就像我手機的指紋解鎖——只有你能開我這道鎖，換一個人試，行不通。

en.content:
"Do you know how fingerprint recognition works?"
"It identifies your unique print?"
"Right. No two are the same in the whole world.
That's what you're like to me —
I can't slot anyone else into the space where you are.
Incompatible. Every time."
en.editorNote: Saying 'you're irreplaceable' through fingerprint logic is more memorable than the direct version — works especially well with tech-savvy people or anyone who unlocks their phone fifty times a day
variations: You're like my phone's fingerprint lock — only you can open this. Anyone else tries, it just doesn't work.

items.sourceNote: 朋朋2026流行語土味情話，原創

zh-tw.content: 「朋朋，我今天可以叫你男女朋友的朋嗎？」
zh-tw.editorNote: 利用2026最流行的稱呼「朋朋」，一句話就把情意說清楚，反差感和趣味性都有；適合輕鬆打破僵局的場合
variations: 朋朋，如果可以的話，我想讓你這個「朋」，升級成「朋友」以上的意思。

en.content: "Hey friend — is it okay if I call you the 'friend' in 'boyfriend' or 'girlfriend'?"
en.editorNote: A play on the 2026 Taiwanese casual term for 'friend' — one line shifts the whole relationship dynamic; works great as a lighthearted icebreaker
variations: If it's okay with you, I'd like to upgrade the definition of our friendship — just by one relationship level.

items.sourceNote: 硬控2026流行語土味情話，原創

zh-tw.content:
「你把我硬控了，完全移不開目光。
醫生說這種症狀沒有藥醫，
只有你能治好。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 「硬控」是2026流行的遊戲用語，指被完全吸引住動不了；把這個詞放進情話，新鮮感十足，懂的人會笑，不懂的人會問，兩種都是好開頭
variations: 你對我施了什麼硬控技能？我試了很久，就是沒辦法無視你。

en.content:
"You've completely locked my attention — I literally cannot look away.
The doctor says there's no prescription for this.
Apparently only you can fix it."
en.editorNote: Using the 2026 gaming slang 'hard control' (being completely held in place by something) as a romantic metaphor — if they know the term they'll laugh; if they don't they'll ask, both are perfect openings
variations: Whatever skill you used on me, it's working. I've tried to look away. It doesn't work.

items.sourceNote: fine-apple / pineapple food pun; rewritten as Taiwan-style 土味情話 dialogue avoiding direct translation

zh-tw.content:
「你知道鳳梨的英文是什麼嗎？」
「pineapple？」
「對。但是如果把 pine 換成 fine，
就變成 fine-apple——
就是你這種人，又甜又出色，
連名字都替你想好了。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 英文諧音梗，適合有點英文底子的對象，說完對方通常要想一秒才反應，然後才笑出來，那個時間差是這個情話最有趣的地方
variations: 你是 fine-apple，不是 pineapple——fine 的那種，不是針的那種。

en.content:
"Do you know the difference between a pineapple and you?"
"No, what?"
"A pineapple is a pine-apple.
You're a fine-apple.
Nature basically already labeled you."
en.editorNote: A classic pun reframed as a comparison rather than a direct label — the setup buys a half-second of genuine confusion before the groan, which makes it funnier
variations: You're not a pineapple. You're a fine-apple. Completely different fruit.

items.sourceNote: strawberry berry attractive food pun; rewritten as Taiwan 土味情話 dialogue with 草莓 framing

zh-tw.content:
「你喜歡草莓嗎？」
「很喜歡啊，為什麼？」
「因為你讓我想到草莓那件事——
不是因為你很甜（雖然你也是），
是因為你讓我完全沒辦法只看一眼就走掉。
草莓也是這樣，一顆就夠讓你停在那邊。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 從對方喜歡草莓這件事切入，把「我忍不住一直看你」說成草莓的魔力，輕鬆自然，在水果攤、超市或甜點店說特別加分
variations: 你比草莓季還難等——草莓每年都會回來，但像你這樣的人，真的不知道等不等得到第二次。

en.content:
"Do you like strawberries?"
"Love them — why?"
"Because you remind me of what strawberries do to people.
Not just the sweet part, though you've got that too.
It's the part where one look is never enough,
and you have to keep coming back for more."
en.editorNote: Using a shared love of strawberries as a launchpad — keeps things light and conversational before landing the compliment; works great at a market or dessert shop
variations: You're harder to wait for than strawberry season — strawberries come back every year. Someone like you, I'm not sure I'd be so lucky twice.

items.sourceNote: black hole gravity space pickup line; rewritten as Taiwan-style 土味情話 with 黑洞 framing avoiding direct copy

zh-tw.content:
「你知道黑洞最厲害的地方是什麼嗎？」
「就是連光都逃不掉？」
「對。但你比黑洞還厲害——
黑洞起碼是因為重力，
你根本不費力，
我就哪裡都去不了了。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 理科的黑洞梗，對方先回答知識點，才意識到是被誇，反應會有點愣，然後才笑，這個延遲感是整個情話的靈魂
variations: 黑洞讓光都無法逃脫，你讓我連想分心都找不到理由。效果是一樣的，但你比較不嚇人。

en.content:
"Do you know what's most impressive about a black hole?"
"That not even light can escape it?"
"Exactly. But honestly, you've got that beat —
a black hole at least needs gravity to work.
You don't try at all,
and I'm still completely stuck."
en.editorNote: The science-question setup pulls them into professor mode, then the pivot catches them off guard — the delayed realization is the whole point; works best delivered deadpan
variations: Black holes trap light without trying. You trap my attention the same way. The physics is similar, but you're significantly less terrifying.

items.sourceNote: broken pencil / pointless classic wordplay; rewritten as Taiwan-style 土味情話 removing direct quote

zh-tw.content:
「你知道一支沒有你的鉛筆有什麼問題嗎？」
「什麼問題？」
「它缺了一個筆尖——沒有了那個，整支就沒有意義了。
我也是一樣，
沒有你在，我幹什麼都覺得沒有重點。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 用鉛筆缺筆尖的比喻說「沒有你，生活失去重心」，帶點冷冷的幽默，對方聽完通常會說「你想太多了」然後忍不住笑
variations: 沒有你的生活就像沒有橡皮擦的鉛筆——不是不能用，但每次犯錯都很難收拾。

en.content:
"You know what's wrong with a pencil that's missing its tip?"
"What?"
"No point.
That's exactly what my days feel like without you —
I'm still going through the motions,
but everything's kind of pointless."
en.editorNote: The classic 'no point' pun reframed as a setup-and-reveal — keeping the wordplay but wrapping it in a quieter, more sincere moment makes it land softer and sweeter
variations: Life without you is like a pencil with no eraser — not unusable, but every mistake feels a lot harder to fix.

items.sourceNote: nebula / lost in beauty space line; rewritten as Taiwan-style 土味情話 with 星雲 framing

zh-tw.content:
「你知道星雲是什麼嗎？」
「就是宇宙裡那種發光的雲狀物？」
「對，而且每一個都長得不一樣，
沒有重複的。
你就是這種感覺——
我在人群裡一眼就認出你，
因為完全沒有第二個這樣的。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 把「你是獨一無二的」說成星雲的特性，比直接誇更有層次，喜歡天文或宇宙感的對象特別買單，說完對方通常會問你怎麼知道星雲的
variations: 星雲在宇宙裡算常見，但每一個形狀都只出現一次。你讓我想到這件事——遇到你，也是只有這一次。

en.content:
"Do you know what a nebula is?"
"Those glowing cloud formations in space?"
"Right — and no two look exactly the same.
Every single one is one of a kind.
That's what you feel like.
In a room full of people, I'd pick you out immediately.
There just isn't a second version of you anywhere."
en.editorNote: Saying 'you're one of a kind' through nebula science gives it texture — the astronomy wrapper makes it feel thoughtful rather than generic; great for someone who loves space or science
variations: Nebulae are everywhere in the universe, but each one only appears once. Meeting you feels the same — this exact version of you, just this once.

items.sourceNote: classic 'cute-cumber' vegetable pun; rewritten as Taiwan-style 土味情話 with a market shopping scenario

zh-tw.content:
「你知道菜市場最難找的是什麼嗎？」
「什麼？」
「就是你這種人。
每次我去買菜，黃瓜小黃瓜都有，
但帥（漂亮）的人只有你一個。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 用菜市場買菜的場景帶出「你是人群裡最獨特的那個」，貼近生活又有點無厘頭，說完對方通常會翻白眼再笑出來
variations: 菜市場裡什麼都有，就是沒有你這個款。

en.content:
"You know what's the hardest thing to find at the grocery store?"
"What?"
"Someone like you.
They've got every vegetable on the shelf,
but a person this a-peel-ing?
That's a rare find."
en.editorNote: Riffs on the classic 'a-peel-ing' fruit/veggie pun but grounds it in a grocery run — the everyday setting makes it feel spontaneous rather than rehearsed, which is half the charm
variations: I could wander every aisle in this store and not find anyone half as interesting as you.

items.sourceNote: Wi-Fi / strong connection tech pun; rewritten with a Taiwan café setting and signal bar metaphor

zh-tw.content:
「你有沒有覺得這間咖啡廳的 Wi-Fi 很奇怪？」
「怎麼了？」
「我一進來訊號就突然滿格了，
但我覺得跟網路沒什麼關係。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 把「見到你心跳加速」轉成 Wi-Fi 訊號滿格的比喻，喜歡咖啡廳約會或對方是科技宅的話特別管用，說完留一秒讓對方反應
variations: 我的手機一靠近你就不斷振動，我懷疑你本人就是一個熱點。

en.content:
"Does the Wi-Fi in here seem weird to you?"
"Why, what's wrong?"
"The moment you walked in, I went from zero bars to full signal.
Pretty sure that's not a router thing."
en.editorNote: Classic Wi-Fi-connection pun but delivered as an observation rather than a direct line — the slight delay before the punchline gives the other person a moment to figure it out, which makes the laugh feel earned
variations: I don't know what network you're running, but you just boosted my signal from one bar to five.

items.sourceNote: chemistry / dangerous reaction pun; rewritten as a lab report framing with Taiwan-style 土味結尾

zh-tw.content:
「我覺得我們之間有很強的化學反應。」
「哦？」
「不是那種會爆炸的——
是那種你加進去之後，
整個實驗結果都變得更好的那種。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 用化學實驗室的比喻說「你讓我變得更好」，理科生或喜歡實驗感的對象特別吃這套，比直接說「你讓我變好」更有畫面感
variations: 我高中化學其實很差，但遇到你之後，我突然覺得我可以重修一次。

en.content:
"I think we've got pretty strong chemistry."
"Oh yeah?"
"Not the explosive kind, don't worry.
More like — the kind where adding you to the equation
makes every result come out better than expected."
en.editorNote: Spins the chemistry pun away from the clichéd 'explosive' angle and toward something gentler — saying 'you make everything better' through lab language feels more sincere and less like a rehearsed line
variations: I was never great at chemistry in school, but something about you makes me want to run the experiment again.

items.sourceNote: Google / 'everything I've been searching for' line; rewritten as a search history scenario with Taiwan internet slang flavour

zh-tw.content:
「你知道我手機的搜尋紀錄都是什麼嗎？」
「什麼？」
「都是我不知道怎麼跟你開口說的那些話。
搜尋了一百次，
最後還是覺得，
直接走過來跟你說比較快。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 把「我一直在想你」說成搜尋紀錄的形式，帶點現代感又不失可愛，對方聽完通常會說「那你搜到答案了嗎」然後你就接「現在有了」
variations: 我 Google 了好幾個版本的自我介紹，但站在你面前，全部都忘光了。

en.content:
"You want to know what my search history looks like?"
"Uh… sure?"
"It's basically a list of everything I kept wanting to say to you
but couldn't figure out how to start.
Eventually I figured —
just walk over here and say it in person."
en.editorNote: Reframes the 'Google / everything I've been searching for' trope as a self-aware, slightly nervous confession — the relatable detail of over-preparing makes it charming rather than slick
variations: I've drafted about twelve different ways to introduce myself to you. This is version thirteen — hope it's the one that works.

items.sourceNote: classic 'living rent-free in my head' modern rizz line; rewritten as a landlord-tenant notice scenario with Taiwan daily-life flavour

zh-tw.content:
「我最近在跟一個房客有糾紛。」
「怎麼了？」
「他住在我腦子裡，
完全不付房租，
而且完全沒有要搬走的意思。」
「……你說的是誰？」
「就是你啊。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 把「你在我腦海裡揮之不去」說成房東房客糾紛，台灣租屋文化讓這個比喻非常貼近生活，說完對方如果回「那你要漲租嗎」就代表他也喜歡你
variations: 我最近失眠，醫生說是因為腦子裡住了一個不肯離開的人，我也不知道要不要請他出去。

en.content:
"I've been having a landlord dispute."
"Oh? With who?"
"Someone who moved into my head without asking,
hasn't paid a single month's rent,
and shows absolutely no sign of leaving."
"…Who are we talking about here?"
"You. Obviously."
en.editorNote: Reframes the 'living rent-free in my head' line as an actual landlord complaint — the deadpan setup before the reveal gives the other person a beat to figure it out, which makes the payoff land better
variations: My brain has had an unauthorised occupant for a while now. Zero rent, zero notice. I should probably evict them — but honestly I don't want to.

items.sourceNote: map / navigation direction pun; rewritten as a phone GPS lost-signal scenario

zh-tw.content:
「我手機的導航最近很怪。」
「怎麼了？」
「我問它怎麼走，它一直叫我往你那個方向走。
走了好幾次，
我覺得它說的有道理。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 用導航 app 把「我想靠近你」說出來，適合在有點距離的場合遞出去，比如對方坐在咖啡廳另一頭或活動另一側，說完可以真的走過去
variations: 我開 Google Maps 查路，它說「目的地在你左邊三步」，我一看，原來就是你。

en.content:
"Something's been wrong with my navigation app."
"What do you mean?"
"Every time I ask it for directions,
it just points me straight toward you.
I've followed it a few times now.
Starting to think it knows something I don't."
en.editorNote: Spins the classic 'I don't need a map' line into a GPS-following bit — framing yourself as just obeying the app takes the pressure off and makes it feel spontaneous rather than rehearsed
variations: I opened my maps app to find somewhere to go tonight. It gave me your coordinates. I'm choosing to trust the algorithm.

items.sourceNote: pasta / spaghetti food pun; rewritten as a cooking show host scenario with Taiwan boba-tea twist

zh-tw.content:
「你知道珍珠奶茶為什麼那麼好喝嗎？」
「因為配方好？」
「因為喝到一半你永遠猜不到下一顆珍珠什麼時候出現，
就像跟你說話，
我永遠不知道你下一句話會讓我笑還是傻住。
結果每次都還是想繼續喝。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 台灣珍珠奶茶幾乎人人有感，把「和你相處充滿驚喜」藏在喝珍珠奶茶的日常體驗裡，既接地氣又不會太肉麻，適合已經聊開的場合
variations: 我跟朋友說我最近迷上一種飲料，朋友問是什麼，我說就是和你說話的感覺——甜的，還會上癮。

en.content:
"You know what makes bubble tea so addictive?"
"The flavor?"
"It's the tapioca pearls —
you never know exactly when the next one's coming,
so you keep sipping just to find out.
Talking to you is exactly like that.
Every time I think I've got you figured out,
you catch me off guard again.
And I can't seem to stop."
en.editorNote: Uses the randomness of boba pearls as a metaphor for delightful unpredictability — it's a compliment disguised as a beverage review, which lands softer than a direct 'you're fascinating'
variations: I told a friend I'd found something I couldn't stop coming back to. They assumed it was a new café. I didn't correct them.

items.sourceNote: cheese / 'grate' food pun; rewritten as a cooking class setting with Taiwan-style self-deprecating humour

zh-tw.content:
「你有沒有覺得我很像起司？」
「……怎麼說？」
「因為我就算放久了也不會變差，
而且只要你在旁邊，
我就覺得什麼料理都變好吃了。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 起司台味十足，用「放久不變差」暗示自己靠得住，再用「讓料理變好吃」說「你讓我變得更好」，兩層意思疊在一起不著痕跡，說完記得笑一下解除尷尬
variations: 我媽說我就像冰箱裡的起司——一直都在，不太起眼，但少了我你會發現哪裡不對。

en.content:
"Has anyone ever told you I'm basically like cheese?"
"…No. Explain."
"I get better the longer I stick around —
and somehow, whenever you're nearby,
everything just tastes better.
I can't fully explain the science of it."
en.editorNote: Flips the 'you're grate' cheese pun by making yourself the cheese — self-deprecating enough to be disarming, but the second half ('everything's better with you around') is genuinely sweet
variations: I'm told I'm an acquired taste. Like aged cheese — not everyone gets it at first, but the people who do tend to stick around.

items.sourceNote: CPR / 'took my breath away' classic line; rewritten as a first-aid certification scenario with deadpan delivery

zh-tw.content:
「我上週剛去考急救證照。」
「真的？很厲害欸。」
「對，但我覺得考試沒有辦法完全準備好。」
「為什麼？」
「題目沒有考到『如果是你讓我呼吸困難，CPR 還有用嗎』這種題型。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 把「你讓我喘不過氣」包在考急救證照的對話裡，設計感比直接說更強，適合已經有點熟識的對象，第一次見面說可能對方會覺得你在開醫學玩笑
variations: 我同學說學 CPR 是為了救人命。我學完之後才發現，有些人光是站在那裡就能把你的心跳搞亂，急救手冊沒有寫怎麼辦。

en.content:
"I just got my first-aid certification last week."
"Oh, that's impressive."
"Thanks. Though I think the course had a gap in the curriculum."
"What do you mean?"
"It never covered what to do when the person causing the breathing problem
is standing right in front of you."
en.editorNote: Wraps the classic 'you took my breath away / hope you know CPR' line inside a first-aid certification anecdote — the slower build-up makes the reveal land harder than just dropping the punchline cold
variations: The manual said to stay calm in an emergency. Nobody warned me that running into you in the hallway would count as one.

items.sourceNote: Riff on the literary 'are you a story / chapter' angle — rebuilt as a Wi-Fi password joke with a slow-build punchline

zh-tw.content:
「不好意思，可以借用一下你家的 Wi-Fi 嗎？」
「可以啊，密碼是什麼你猜猜看？」
「我覺得應該是『我喜歡你』。」
「為什麼？」
「因為連到你身上之後，我就再也不想斷線了。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 用借 Wi-Fi 當開場白，把「連線」雙關成感情的連結，最後一句故意說得很慢效果最好，適合咖啡廳或共同空間第一次搭話
variations: 你家網路訊號特別強欸，難怪我每次經過都會自動連上。
我手機說偵測到一個叫『心動』的熱點，原來訊號源是你。

en.content:
"Hey, mind if I borrow your Wi-Fi for a sec?"
"Sure. Want to guess the password?"
"I'm going to say it's 'I like you.'"
"Why's that?"
"Because once I connected to you, I never wanted to log off."
en.editorNote: Wi-Fi-as-romance metaphor reframed as a request — the slow reveal of the 'password' lands better than blurting the punchline, works best in cafes or coworking spaces
variations: Your signal must be premium — my phone keeps auto-connecting whenever I walk past.
My phone flagged a network called 'heart racing.' Turns out the source was just you.

items.sourceNote: Adapted from the 'you're a memorable quote' literary angle — recast as a bookstore browsing scenario

zh-tw.content:
「你最近有在看什麼書嗎？」
「有啊，最近在讀一本散文集。」
「我也是。」
「真的？什麼書？」
「書名叫『你』，作者不詳，每天讀都有新發現。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 把對方比喻成一本讀不完的書，比直接說「你很有趣」高級很多，適合在書店、圖書館或聊到閱讀話題時使用，記得最後一句要看著對方眼睛說
variations: 我在找一本書找很久了，書名叫『跟你聊一整晚』，圖書館一直缺貨。
聽說你也在看書？那我們交換書單好了，我那本第一頁就寫你的名字。

en.content:
"Reading anything good lately?"
"Yeah, working through a collection of essays."
"Same here, actually."
"Oh? What's it called?"
"It's titled 'You.' Author unknown. I find something new every time I open it."
en.editorNote: Casts the other person as a book you keep rereading — much more elegant than 'you're interesting,' best deployed in a bookstore, library, or once reading comes up naturally; hold eye contact for the last line
variations: I've been hunting for a book called 'A Whole Night of Conversation With You.' Library says it's always checked out.
We should swap reading lists. Mine has your name on page one.

items.sourceNote: Reworked from the 'is your dad a boxer / you're a knockout' tradition — replaced with a coffee-barista misorder scenario

zh-tw.content:
「不好意思，這杯咖啡好像點錯了。」
「怎麼了？我幫你重做。」
「我點的是美式，但喝起來特別甜。」
「奇怪，我沒加糖啊。」
「那大概是因為你剛剛幫我點單的時候笑了一下。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 情境型土味情話，把對方的笑容當作糖的來源，比直接稱讚對方笑容好看更有畫面感，適合咖啡店打工的對象或熟客間互撩
variations: 你們家咖啡是不是換豆子了？我覺得最近喝起來特別療癒，仔細想想是因為你都在櫃台。
我以為我點錯了，後來發現是你站在那裡讓整杯咖啡都變甜。

en.content:
"Sorry, I think there's something wrong with this coffee."
"Oh no, let me remake it for you."
"I ordered an Americano, but it tastes kind of sweet."
"That's weird, I didn't add any sugar."
"Then it must be because you smiled while ringing me up."
en.editorNote: Situational pickup that credits the smile for the sweetness — far more vivid than 'you have a nice smile,' great for repeat customers at a cafe or anyone working a counter
variations: Did this place change beans? Coffee's been hitting different — though I just realized you've been on shift every time.
Thought my order got mixed up. Turns out you were just standing there and the whole cup turned sweet.

items.sourceNote: Built around the modern 'rizz / smooth lines' style — uses a phone notification framing for self-aware delivery

zh-tw.content:
「我手機剛剛跳出一個提醒。」
「什麼提醒？」
「它說『電池剩 1%，請立即充電』。」
「那你趕快去充啊。」
「不行，最近的充電器在你那裡。」
「我哪有你的充電器？」
「你的笑容啊，我一看到就會自動回血。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 用手機沒電的日常場景帶入，最後把笑容比喻成充電器，整段節奏要慢，前面越像在閒聊後面反差越大，適合朋朋之間玩鬧型告白
variations: 我最近設定了一個 Siri 捷徑，只要看到你就自動進入省電模式以外的所有模式。
我手機 App 通知一直提醒我『該找你聊聊了』，我也不知道哪個 App 這麼懂我。

en.content:
"My phone just popped up a notification."
"What did it say?"
"'Battery at 1%. Please charge immediately.'"
"Then go plug it in."
"I can't. The nearest charger is with you."
"I don't have your charger."
"Your smile. One look and I'm back to a hundred percent."
en.editorNote: Builds from a mundane low-battery moment into a smile-as-charger reveal — pacing matters, the more casual the setup sounds, the harder the punchline lands; ideal for playful banter between friends sliding toward more
variations: I set up a Siri shortcut that turns off low-power mode the second I see you.
My phone keeps nudging me with a reminder that says 'go talk to them.' Honestly, no idea which app got that smart.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by the magician 'everyone else disappears' archetype, reframed as a movie-theater scene

zh-tw.content:
「欸，剛剛電影裡那個變魔術的好厲害。」
「對啊，他把整個東西變不見了。」
「沒有你厲害啦。」
「我哪有變什麼？」
「你一坐到我旁邊，整間電影院的人就全部消失了，我眼裡只剩你。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 把魔術師的『讓人消失』比喻挪到電影院場景，前面假裝在閒聊電影，後面才轉進來，適合第一次一起看電影的時候用，記得要假裝隨口一提才有效
variations: 你一定偷學過魔術，不然怎麼一抬頭就把全班同學變不見，只剩我看著你。
你坐到我旁邊那一秒，背景就模糊了，這是哪一款電影濾鏡你教教我。

en.content:
"That magician in the movie was unreal."
"Yeah, he made the whole thing vanish."
"Still not as good as you, though."
"I didn't make anything disappear."
"You sat down next to me and the entire theater emptied out. I can only see you."
en.editorNote: Reframes the classic 'magician makes everyone disappear' line inside a real movie-theater moment — works best on a first movie night, deliver it like a passing comment, not a setup
variations: You must've taken magic lessons. One look up and the whole class is gone — just you in the frame.
The second you sat down, the background went out of focus. What filter are you running?

items.sourceNote: Adapted from the 'Cu-Te / copper + tellurium' chemistry pun, swapped to a different element pairing

zh-tw.content:
「你高中化學有念嗎？」
「有啊，怎麼了？」
「那你知道氟（F）加上你（U）等於什麼嗎？」
「等於什麼？」
「等於 FU——我對你的 fu 很重。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 用化學元素拼成『fu』的諧音梗，要先確認對方聽得懂中文裡『fu』的意思（感覺/喜歡），理組朋友會邊翻白眼邊笑，非常適合宅宅之間互撩
variations: 你知道為什麼週期表裡沒有『我們』這個元素嗎？因為它已經跟你結合掉了。
化學課老師說同性相斥異性相吸，那我可以申請當你的異電荷嗎？

en.content:
"Did you take chemistry in high school?"
"Yeah, why?"
"Then you know what you get when you combine fluorine (F) and uranium (U)?"
"What?"
"FU — and that's exactly the energy I'm catching from you."
en.editorNote: A chemistry pun built on element symbols spelling something flirty — works best on someone who'll roll their eyes before they laugh, perfect nerd-on-nerd banter at a study session
variations: Why isn't 'us' on the periodic table? Because it already bonded with you.
Chemistry says opposites attract. Can I file paperwork to be your opposite charge?

items.sourceNote: Built from the 'wedding RSVP' style line that's been trending on Dcard, reframed as a casual chat

zh-tw.content:
「我下個月有個重要的場合，你一定要來。」
「什麼場合？這麼正式？」
「我婚禮。」
「蛤？你要結婚了？跟誰？」
「還沒決定，但是新郎/新娘那邊如果不是你坐，那個位子會很空。」
zh-tw.editorNote: Dcard 上爆紅的婚禮邀請梗，重點在前兩句故意裝認真嚇一下對方，最後再轉折回來，使用前要確認關係已經有點曖昧，不然會有點驚嚇
variations: 我以後拍婚紗的時候，攝影師問我女主角/男主角是誰，我大概只能默默指你。
我幫我們的婚禮場地都先看好了，就等你確認一下你哪天有空。

en.content:
"I've got a big event next month. You have to come."
"What event? Sounds serious."
"My wedding."
"Wait, what? You're getting married? To who?"
"Haven't picked yet. But if you're not standing across from me, that spot's gonna look really empty."
en.editorNote: A trending wedding-invite line — the trick is selling the first two beats with a straight face so the pivot lands, only deploy when there's already some flirty tension or it'll spook them
variations: When I do the engagement photoshoot one day, the photographer's gonna ask who the other person is. I think I'll just point at you.
I already scouted out venues for our wedding. Just need you to check your calendar.

items.sourceNote: Riffs on the 'time traveler / I see you in my future' line, set inside a fortune-teller framing

zh-tw.content:
「我昨天去算命，算命師講了一句很妙的話。」
「他說什麼？」
「他說我未來會遇到一個對的人。」
「然後呢？」
「他叫我把手機拿出來，翻到通訊錄第一頁——然後他指著你的名字。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 把『我在未來看到你』那種老梗包進算命師的場景，畫面感強很多，最後一句要慢慢講，配合滑手機的動作會更可愛，適合 LINE 聊天熱絡之後當作見面收尾
variations: 算命師說我的姻緣已經出現在我的最近聯絡人裡了，我滑了半天才確定一定是你。
他叫我許三個願望，我講了兩個就被打斷，他說剩下那個你會親自幫我實現。

en.content:
"I went to a fortune teller yesterday and she said the wildest thing."
"What'd she say?"
"That I was gonna meet 'the one' soon."
"Okay, and?"
"She told me to open my phone, scroll to the top of my contacts — and pointed straight at your name."
en.editorNote: A storytelling spin on the classic 'I see you in my future' line — the fortune-teller framing makes it cinematic, deliver the last beat slowly while miming the phone scroll for full effect
variations: She said my person was already in my recent contacts. Took me a few swipes, but I'm pretty sure it's you.
She let me make three wishes. Got through two before she stopped me — said you'd be the one granting the third.

items.sourceNote: Built from the trending TikTok 'For You page' / app-themed pickup line, reframed into a short conversational beat

zh-tw.content:
「我最近覺得我的演算法壞掉了。」
「為什麼？」
「不管我滑什麼，最後都會想到你。連推薦給我的食譜都讓我想揪你吃飯。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 把 TikTok 演算法梗包成情話，給有在用社群的人最有共鳴，適合 IG 私訊或 LINE 上的閒聊延伸
variations: 我手機的探索頁全部都是你會喜歡的東西，我覺得它在偷偷暗示我什麼。
演算法說它推給我的下一個會讓我心動，結果跳出來的是你的限動。

en.content:
"I think my algorithm is broken."
"How come?"
"Doesn't matter what I scroll — it always loops back to you. Even the recipes it pushes me make me wanna invite you to dinner."
en.editorNote: Modern app-era pickup riffing on TikTok's For You page — best on someone who lives a little online, drop it as a follow-up DM after liking a few of their stories
variations: My explore page is just stuff you'd like. I'm pretty sure my phone is trying to tell me something.
The algorithm promised the next post would make my heart skip. Of course it was your story.

items.sourceNote: Coffee-shop framing built from the classic 'you're my type' wordplay, restructured as a barista mini-skit

zh-tw.content:
「不好意思，可以幫我推薦一下嗎？我不知道要點什麼。」
「你平常喜歡什麼？」
「我喜歡的這家好像沒有賣。」
「沒有賣什麼？」
「沒有賣你。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 咖啡廳場景的小劇場，前面假裝認真問飲料，最後一句直接送上甜點等級的暴擊，適合在咖啡店或飲料店初次破冰
variations: 店員問我要哪一款，我說我要一杯加了你的。
菜單我看半天都不知道要點什麼，因為我喜歡的不在上面，是站在櫃檯前的你。

en.content:
"Sorry — could you recommend something? I have no idea what to get."
"What do you usually like?"
"Looks like this place doesn't carry it."
"Doesn't carry what?"
"You."
en.editorNote: A coffee-shop bit that plays it straight on the menu question before the soft pivot — works as a low-stakes first-meet line, the kind that earns a laugh even if it doesn't land a number
variations: The barista asked which one I wanted. I said the one with you in it.
I've stared at this menu for ten minutes. Turns out my type isn't on it — they're behind the counter.

items.sourceNote: Reworks the 'are you a library book / I'm checking you out' line into a Spotify / playlist context that feels current

zh-tw.content:
「我新做了一個歌單，叫『不能跳過的歌』。」
「裡面有什麼？」
「裡面只有一首歌，重複播放。」
「真的假的？哪一首？」
「沒有那首歌，是你傳給我的那則訊息。我已經看了一整天還在重播。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 把『單曲循環』的概念用在訊息上，比直接說『我一直想你』高級一點，用在已經開始日常聊天但還沒告白的階段最好
variations: 我手機今天最常打開的不是音樂，是你的對話框，那個比歌還洗腦。
我以為我在循環的是某首歌，後來發現是你昨天跟我說的那句晚安。

en.content:
"I made a new playlist called 'Songs I Can't Skip.'"
"What's on it?"
"Just one track. On loop."
"Wait, really? Which song?"
"There's no song. It's that text you sent me earlier. I've been replaying it in my head all day."
en.editorNote: An updated 'you're stuck in my head' line via the on-loop playlist trope — best at the texting-but-not-official stage, the soft swerve sells the sincerity
variations: The most-played thing on my phone today isn't a song. It's our chat window.
I thought I had a song stuck in my head. Turned out it was just you saying goodnight.

items.sourceNote: Riffs on the viral 'are you a filter, because you make my day better' angle, reframed as a phone-camera bit

zh-tw.content:
「欸，你幫我看一下我的相機是不是壞了。」
「怎麼了？看起來正常啊。」
「不正常，鏡頭只要對到你，整個畫面就特別亮。」
「那是因為光線吧？」
「不是，是因為我們之間，連光都比較喜歡你。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 用手機相機當道具的小情境，最後一句講『連光都比較喜歡你』很容易讓對方笑出來，建議邊講邊真的舉起手機，效果會加倍
variations: 我的相機今天電量掉超快，因為它一直忙著把你拍清楚。
我前鏡頭壞了，後鏡頭只願意對著你，可能它比我還誠實。

en.content:
"Hey, can you check if my camera's broken?"
"Looks fine to me."
"It's not. Whenever I point it at you, the whole frame lights up."
"That's just the lighting."
"Nope. It's because even the light in this room has a favorite — and it's you."
en.editorNote: Phone-camera framing on the classic 'you light up the room' line — actually hold up your phone while delivering it, the prop sells the bit and lands the laugh
variations: My battery's been dying way faster today. I think the camera's been working overtime trying to capture you.
Front cam's broken. Back cam only wants to point at you. Even my phone has standards.

items.sourceNote: Reworks the viral 'are you a trending topic' angle into a演算法情境梗

zh-tw.content:
「我覺得我的演算法壞掉了。」
「怎麼說？」
「我滑首頁，每一篇都讓我想到你。」
「那不就是你自己一直搜尋我？」
「沒有，我什麼都沒搜，是這個世界自己覺得我該想你。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 把『我一直想你』包裝成演算法在替自己背書，網路世代的情侶或剛在交友軟體聊起來的對象很吃這套
variations: 我以為我有開隱身，結果你還是出現在我每一頁的探索，可能是緣分忘記關通知。
我手機建議的關鍵字最近全部都跟你有關，我懷疑它比我還早承認喜歡你。

en.content:
"I think my algorithm is broken."
"How come?"
"Every post on my feed somehow reminds me of you."
"Maybe you've been searching for me?"
"Nope. I haven't searched a thing. The internet just decided on its own that I should be thinking about you."
en.editorNote: Reframes 'I keep thinking about you' as the algorithm vouching for it — lands well with the dating-app crowd or anyone who lives on their feed
variations: I had ghost mode on and you still showed up on my For You page. Pretty sure fate forgot to log out.
My autocomplete keeps suggesting your name. I think my phone caught feelings before I did.

items.sourceNote: Coffee-shop framing on the classic 'you're my type' wordplay

zh-tw.content:
「不好意思，可以幫我點一杯跟你一樣的嗎？」
「我又不是飲料。」
「我知道，但我想試試看『有點甜、又會讓我心跳加速』是什麼味道。」
「店員聽到會笑你欸。」
「沒關係，反正我已經被你笑了一整天。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合咖啡廳、手搖店第一次見面或約會的場景，把對方比喻成一杯飲料，最後自嘲收尾比較不油膩
variations: 我不太會點飲料，可是我看到你就知道，我這輩子應該都只想點這一杯。
菜單我看不懂，但你的笑容是我今天唯一看得懂的甜度。

en.content:
"Excuse me, can I order whatever you're having?"
"I'm not a drink."
"I know. I just want to know what 'a little sweet and definitely bad for my heart rate' tastes like."
"The barista's gonna laugh at you."
"That's fine. You've been making me laugh at myself all day anyway."
en.editorNote: Cafe or boba-shop opener — comparing the person to a drink, then closing with self-deprecation keeps it from feeling oily
variations: I'm bad at picking drinks, but one look at you and I'm pretty sure I'd order the same thing for the rest of my life.
I can't read the menu, but your smile is the only sweetness level I understood today.

items.sourceNote: Smooth rizz framing on the timeless 'do you have a map' line

zh-tw.content:
「不好意思，可以借我看一下你的手機嗎？」
「幹嘛？」
「我想用一下你的地圖。」
「你要去哪裡？」
「我不確定，但我從看到你那一刻起，就一直在迷路。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 經典『可以借我地圖嗎，因為我在你眼睛裡迷路了』的本土化加長版，加一個『借手機』的小動作會更自然，適合朋友聚會或聯誼場合
variations: 你眼睛太亮，我每次看一下都要重新校正方向，到底是誰把導航設成你的。
我手機定位壞掉了，可是我發現只要看著你，我就知道我該往哪裡走。

en.content:
"Hey, can I borrow your phone for a sec?"
"For what?"
"I need to pull up a map."
"Where are you headed?"
"Not sure yet. I've been kind of lost ever since I looked at you."
en.editorNote: Modern reboot of the classic 'I got lost in your eyes' line — adding the 'borrow your phone' setup grounds it in a real moment, works great at parties or group hangs
variations: Your eyes are so bright I have to recalibrate every time I look. Whoever set my GPS to you owes me an explanation.
My phone's location is glitching, but somehow whenever I look at you I know exactly which way to go.
